verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Surfline and Surf-Forecast agree that the useful windows are poor.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY looks like no reliable window, and it’s probably not worth chasing. Rey Cortiço at lunch is the best of what’s available, but the swell is tiny and the wind setup for that spot doesn’t look great. Before you leave, I’d check Surfline and the MEO Beachcam—unless the cams surprise us with cleaner, more rideable waves, I’d save the drive.

best.windFit: NNW 15 km/h · onshore · poor wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 15 kJ, 10 s period, lully 1/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 10-13s at low tide can still work well on Belgas.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot; exposed north spot probably too small.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
exposed north spot probably too small
